channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Israel says plan to expand into Lebanon will provide 'defensive buffer'
item.description: Israel signals major expansion into Lebanon, with plans to control swathes of southern Lebanon in a bid for a "defensive buffer."
item.content:encoded: Israel signals major expansion into Lebanon, with plans to control swathes of southern Lebanon in a bid for a "defensive buffer."
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: Ex-peace negotiator for Israel on country's goals as it expands into Lebanon
item.description: What are Israel's larger goals as it expands its offensive into Lebanon? NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Daniel Levy, a former peace negotiator for Israel.
item.content:encoded: What are Israel's larger goals as it expands its offensive into Lebanon? NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Daniel Levy, a former peace negotiator for Israel.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: A California sheriff is probing an election fraud claim. The AG calls it baseless
item.description: A firebrand sheriff, running as a Republican for governor of California, has seized hundreds of thousands of ballots in an election fraud probe. California's attorney general says there's no evidence.
item.content:encoded: A firebrand sheriff, running as a Republican for governor of California, has seized hundreds of thousands of ballots in an election fraud probe. California's attorney general says there's no evidence.
item.dc:creator: Madison Aument

item.title: A shelter village provides a bridge to permanent housing
item.description: Shelter villages offer temporary and private places for the unhoused to sleep and store belongings. One of the newest, The Bridge, opened recently in central Illinois.
item.content:encoded:
Shelter villages offer temporary and private places for the unhoused to sleep and store belongings. One of the newest, The Bridge, opened recently in central Illinois.

(Image credit: Emily Bollinger)
item.dc:creator: Emily Bollinger

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Israel signals a major expansion into Lebanon, talks to fund DHS and fully reopen the government hit snags, NTSB shares early findings in LaGuardia Airport crash.
item.content:encoded: Israel signals a major expansion into Lebanon, talks to fund DHS and fully reopen the government hit snags, NTSB shares early findings in LaGuardia Airport crash.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Sofia Coppola turns the lens on designer Marc Jacobs for her first documentary
item.description: NPR's A Martínez speaks with director Sofia Coppola about her new documentary "Marc by Sofia," which looks at the rise of influential designer Marc Jacobs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez speaks with director Sofia Coppola about her new documentary "Marc by Sofia," which looks at the rise of influential designer Marc Jacobs.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Islandwide blackouts bring life in Cuba to a near total halt
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
item.description: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.
item.content:encoded: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Pablo Picasso painting to be raffled off to benefit Alzheimer's research
item.description: Pablo Picasso's grandson is raffling off one of his grandfather's paintings — valued at more than $1 million — for 100 euros ($116) a ticket to benefit Alzheimer's research.
item.content:encoded: Pablo Picasso's grandson is raffling off one of his grandfather's paintings — valued at more than $1 million — for 100 euros ($116) a ticket to benefit Alzheimer's research.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Pentagon orders 2,000 soldiers to deploy to the Middle East, source confirms
item.description: A U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly confirmed to NPR that the Pentagon has ordered some 2,000 soldiers from the Army's 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: A U.S. official who was not authorized to speak publicly confirmed to NPR that the Pentagon has ordered some 2,000 soldiers from the Army's 82nd Airborne Division to deploy to the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence
